title: MailerLite vs Substack (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare MailerLite and Substack. See pricing, features, and which email platform suits your needs.
intro: MailerLite and Substack are both email marketing platforms, but they serve very different purposes. MailerLite is a traditional email marketing tool designed for businesses seeking automation, landing pages, and customer engagement, while Substack is a newsletter platform built for writers and creators to monetize their audience directly. Your choice depends on whether you need business marketing features or a platform optimized for paid subscriptions.
choose_a_if: You need advanced email automation and marketing workflows for your business
You want to build landing pages, pop-ups, and surveys to grow your audience
You're selling products or services and need e-commerce integrations
You prefer transparent per-subscriber pricing over revenue-share models
You need professional customer support and established integrations
choose_b_if: You're a writer or creator looking to monetize your newsletter directly
You want unlimited subscribers on a free plan with no subscriber limits
You're interested in hosting podcasts alongside your newsletter
You prefer a simple, distraction-free platform for publishing content
You want access to Substack's built-in audience and discovery features

faq.a: It depends on your use case. MailerLite is better for businesses needing marketing automation, landing pages, and e-commerce features. Substack is better for writers and creators focused on building and monetizing a paid subscriber base. MailerLite has higher customer satisfaction ratings (4.6 vs 4.0 on G2), but Substack excels for content creators.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, MailerLite or Substack?
faq.a: Substack is free with unlimited subscribers—you only pay if you earn revenue from paid subscriptions (10% commission). MailerLite is free up to 1,000 subscribers, then starts at $9/month. For small audiences, Substack is typically cheaper; for growing businesses, MailerLite's fixed pricing may be more predictable.

faq.q: Can I switch from Substack to MailerLite?
faq.a: Yes, you can export your subscriber list from Substack and import it into MailerLite. However, you'll lose Substack's paid subscription features and built-in monetization. The switch is straightforward, but consider whether MailerLite's business tools align better with your needs before migrating.
